content.text:
`formatRelative` is imported/called only in `webclient/src/components/UserBadge.tsx`, so this is the sole call site.

1. **File & symbol responsible:**
   - `webclient/src/utils/dates.ts` — the `formatRelative(iso: string, now: Date = new Date()): string` function (lines ~6-12). This is the single source of truth that builds the `"3h ago"` style string via manual bucket math (`< 60` → `just now`, `< 3600` → `${m}m ago`, `< 86400` → `${h}h ago`, else `${d}d ago`).
   - `webclient/src/components/UserBadge.tsx` — the only consumer, imports `formatRelative` from `../utils/dates` and renders `joined {formatRelative(joinedAt)}`.

2. **Mechanism / fix plan:**
   The abbreviated units (`m`, `h`, `d`) are hardcoded as template-literal suffixes inside `formatRelative` in `dates.ts`. Because there's exactly one producer function and one call site in the whole repo (confirmed via reference/caller search — no other component or util builds an "ago" string), the fix is localized: rewrite the return strings in `formatRelative` to use the full words, e.g.:
   ```ts
   if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)} minute${...} ago`;
   if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)} hour${...} ago`;
   return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)} day${...} ago`;
   ```
   (reusing the existing `pluralize` helper from `webclient/src/utils/format.ts` would keep singular/plural handling consistent, e.g. `pluralize(hours, 'hour')`). Since `UserBadge.tsx` is the only place that calls `formatRelative`, changing this one function updates the display "everywhere" in the web client — no other files need touching.

  Note: there's an analogous but separate Python helper, `humanize_delta` in `pyserver/utils/time.py`, which produces the same short style (`3h`, `2d`) — but it's server-side, not part of the web client, so it's out of scope unless the request is meant to cover backend-rendered strings too.
